title: Commissioner for the Tenant Farming Sector: role and services
body: "Alan Laidlaw is our Commissioner for the Tenant Farming Sector in England.  Role  The commissioner  promotes the standards of conduct and responsible behaviour that are set out in the voluntary industry Agricultural Landlord and Tenant Code of Practice in England.  Raise an issue  This provides a confidential and impartial point of contact for anyone in the sector to raise concerns. These concerns may be regarding unreasonable behaviour, poor conduct, and non-compliance with the code of practice.   Contact information  You can get in touch with the commissioner’s office at CTFS@defra.gov.uk.  Background  Defra appointed a Commissioner for the Tenant Farming Sector following a recommendation in the independent Rock Review, to encourage collaboration and fairness within the tenanted sector. "
